ignore-generated-files.label: Keep Generated Files Out of History
ignore-generated-files.summary: Add one generated file, introduce one ignore rule, and stop tracking generated output safely.

sketch2.title: Tracked Files Need an Extra Step

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.title: Add a Generated Summary Once
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.prompt: Create `community-project/generated/summary.txt`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly three lines: `Generated summary`, a blank line, and `Created automatically from project notes.` Save it. Inspect short status, stage the file, and commit it with the message `Add generated summary`.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.hint: Create the supplied generated file and record it so the next lesson can correct that tracking decision.

help.concept: Git can track any text file, even one that can be regenerated. This step intentionally records the summary so you can later learn why an ignore rule alone does not stop tracking an existing path.
help.hint_1: Do not create any other generated file yet.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.solutionCode:
git status --short
git add generated/summary.txt
git commit -m "Add generated summary"
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch0.instructions: Create `community-project/generated/summary.txt`. Starting on line 1, enter exactly three lines: `Generated summary`, a blank line, and `Created automatically from project notes.` Save it. Inspect short status, stage the file, and commit it with the message `Add generated summary`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Start community project'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-02T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Add community contacts'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-03T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community schedule'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0130
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-04T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Update community contacts'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/events.txt
git -C community-project add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-05T12:00:00Z git -C community-project commit -q -m 'Organize community project files'
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/events.txt
git -C community-project add -A
find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 9:30 AM
' > community-project/events.txt

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you inspect the new generated summary?

1.message: Did you stage only the generated summary?

2.message: Did you record the generated summary once?

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.title: Apply the Ignore Rule to Tracked Output
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.prompt: Create `community-project/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it. Stage `.gitignore`, then stop tracking `generated/summary.txt` without deleting its local file. Inspect status with ignored paths included. Do not commit yet.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.hint: Track the policy, remove only the summary’s index entry, and verify that the local file is now ignored.

help.concept: A `.gitignore` line containing `generated/` matches untracked files in that folder. Because `summary.txt` was already tracked, `git rm --cached generated/summary.txt` is also required. `--cached` removes the index entry while preserving the working file.
help.hint_1: Do not use plain `git rm`, which would delete the local file.
help.hint_2: The ignored summary should appear with `!!` when ignored paths are shown.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.solutionCode:
git add .gitignore
git rm --cached generated/summary.txt
git status --short --ignored
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch2.instructions: Create `community-project/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it. Stage `.gitignore`, then stop tracking `generated/summary.txt` without deleting its local file. Inspect status with ignored paths included. Do not commit yet.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.title: Record the Ignore Policy
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.prompt: The ignore policy and generated-file untracking are staged. Record them with the message `Ignore generated output`, then confirm that normal short status is clean.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.hint: Commit the prepared policy change and verify the tracked state.

help.concept: `git commit -m "Ignore generated output"` records `.gitignore` and removes the generated summary from the current tracked snapshot. The local summary remains on disk, ignored by the new rule.
help.hint_2: Normal short status should produce no entries.

try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Ignore generated output"
git status --short
try_ignore_generated_files_sketch3.instructions: The ignore policy and generated-file untracking are staged. Record them with the message `Ignore generated output`, then confirm that normal short status is clean.

mkdir -p -- community-project

git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'

find community-project -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 ! -name .git -exec rm -rf -- {} +
mkdir -p -- community-project

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you record the ignore policy and tracked-file cleanup?

1.message: Did you verify that normal status is clean?

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.title: Purpose of .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.prompt: What is the primary purpose of a `.gitignore` file in a Git repository?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-0.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To track changes in all files
options.b: To ignore specific files and directories
options.c: To delete files from the repository
options.d: To rename files in the repository

ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.title: Effect of Adding to .gitignore
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.prompt: What happens when you add a file to `.gitignore` that has already been committed?
ignore-generated-files-single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The file is automatically deleted from the repository
options.b: The file is untracked and removed from history
options.c: The file remains tracked and in history
options.d: The file is renamed in the repository

ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.title: Useful Ignore Patterns
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.prompt: Which patterns match generated or local-only files used in this course? Select all that apply.
ignore-generated-files-multi-choice-0.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.title: Stop Tracking a Local-Only File
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.prompt: Arrange the safe steps for a file that is already tracked but should remain only on each developer’s machine.
ignore-generated-files-drag-reorder-0.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Write narrow ignore rules, prove which rule matches, and separate “ignored” from “already tracked.”
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t2: Remove the path from Git’s index while keeping the working copy

ignore-generated-files-sketch0.title: See Why Generated Output Needs a Policy
ignore-generated-files-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: Git can track a generated text file just like any other file. After creating the exact file, `git status --short` identifies it, `git add generated/summary.txt` stages it, and `git commit -m "Add generated summary"` records it once. The next steps correct that tracking choice.

ignore-generated-files-sketch2.title: Ignore New Generated Files
ignore-generated-files-sketch2.bodyMarkdown: A `.gitignore` line containing `generated/` matches untracked files inside that folder. Stage the policy with `git add .gitignore`, then use `git rm --cached generated/summary.txt` because the summary is already tracked. `git status --short --ignored` verifies that the preserved local summary is now ignored.

ignore-generated-files-sketch3.title: Stop Tracking Without Deleting
ignore-generated-files-sketch3.bodyMarkdown: After the rule and untracking action are staged, `git commit -m "Ignore generated output"` records them together. `git status --short` should then be empty even though the ignored summary remains available locally.
